title: Data Scientist/Assoc. Dir., technical lead, protein discovery

notes.text: Led ElevateBio's supervised learning strategy for protein optimization:
children: Protein language model embeddings as features with supervised heads predicting fitness.
Regularized and gradient-boosted regressors over NGS/deep-mutational-scanning fitness readouts.
Active learning and Bayesian optimization to select each design–build–test round's variant library.

notes.text: Developed the training dataset for and built a high-performing XGBoost classifier on Evo2 genomic language model embeddings, resolving mobile-genetic-element vs. host by (meta)genomic contig position.

Built a self-improving agentic system (Anthropic SDK) that ingests and reformats Excel submissions, validating output against a Pydantic-style schema and feeding successes into a few-shot example bank.
Led ElevateBio's supervised learning strategy for protein optimization from the dry-lab side.
Built containerized NGS pipelines (Nextflow, AWS Batch) to process sequencing data at scale.
Built ML training/eval infrastructure: GPU compute, containerized inference jobs, experiment tracking.
Built the reporting stack: Dash dashboards on Fargate, nginx-proxied static HTML reports stored on S3.
Developed the company ML benchmarking and active learning software.
Led the development of ElevateBio's protein catalog for therapeutic discovery.

variants.platform: Led development of ElevateBio's protein catalog: the database, search, and API layer behind therapeutic protein discovery.

children.text: Provisioned its AWS infrastructure with CDK: networking, IAM, Batch, Fargate, and S3.

title: Data Scientist/Engineer and Data Science Group Leader

notes.text: First author on AgBiome's first peer-reviewed publication — an Editor's Pick in Phytobiomes Journal: multivariate and compositional statistical analysis of 16S/metagenomic surveys resolving sweetpotato microbiome community assembly.
Designed and led AgBiome's AWS Genomics Data Platform: an event-driven system of genomics pipelines and developer tooling feeding a queryable database that informed R&D.
Developed cloud-based infrastructure and data scientist developer tooling to write and deploy data-driven monitoring and real-time analytics dashboards.

title: Lead Faculty, Course Developer
organization: UNC—Gillings SPH and Sch. of Data and Info. Sciences

notes.text: Develop and teach three courses across the Gillings School of Public Health and the School of Data and Information Sciences:
children: AI/ML and statistical methods for studying biology (DATA-791).

title: Postdoctoral Researcher and Research Associate
notes: Designed and applied a microbiome analysis pipeline for DNA-SIP-based microbial ecology studies of agricultural soils and biological soil crusts.
Published several manuscripts as first author in refereed journals and contributed as a co-author to many additional scientific publications.

title: Teaching Fellow Microbial Diversity Course, Four Summers
notes: Taught data analysis best practices in microbiome science, molecular biology laboratory techniques and administered the course's Linux servers.

title: SULI Undergraduate Researcher, Two Summers
notes: Two summers studying thermophilic microorganisms at INL. Awarded SULI undergraduate scholarship.
